title: Plant Defences
editorialSummary: Plants grouped by their primary defence strategy against herbivores or pathogens; the false trail is that both stinging nettles and cacti have physical deterrents, but nettles use chemical-tipped hollow hairs (a chemical-physical hybrid) while cacti use purely structural spines — the puzzle places stinging nettle in the chemical group because its defensive effect is entirely dependent on the formic-acid injection, not the hair structure alone. Passionflower mimics Heliconius butterfly eggs on tendrils to deter oviposition; Wild Ginger mimics leaf litter to deter herbivores.
